editions.label: An Edition and Study of the Gos̱iga-sutra, the Cow-Horn Discourse: An Account of the Harmonious Aṇarudha Monks

lemmas.translation: as far as, up to, whenever
the base of the end of space

sequences.label: Paragraph 1
sequences.translation: (Thus have I heard. On one occasion the Blessed One was living at Nadika in the brick house ... Now on that occasion Venerable Aṇarudha and Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira were living in a Grove of the Cow-Horn Śāla-tree Forest).

sequences.label: Paragraph 2
sequences.translation: ... they established.
sequences.chaya: [1] ... . pratisthāpayensuḥ (BHS) ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 3
sequences.translation: [2] (Whichever of those venerables) returns (first from the village for the sake of seeking alms-food), he [3] (approaches the refectory and, having approached, he prepares the seats), he sets out the (drinking water pitcher), [and] sets out the plates, he gets the water for washing from the water basin, (... he sets out the leftovers container. Having put aside any leftovers, he eats) [4] [and] departs.
sequences.chaya: [2] ... pratikramati saḥ yena [3] ... pānīyaghaṭam upasthāpayati pātrīḥ upasthāpayati *udakaraḍim udapātrāt asthāsi (BHS) [4] prakramati ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 4
sequences.translation: Whichever of those venerables (returns) last [5] (from the village for the sake of seeking) alms, (if there is enough food in his bowl, he eats it. If) there is (not enough), he takes from the leftovers container [6] (and eats it. He puts the seats away in their right place); he puts (the drinking water pot) away (in its right place); he (puts) the washing water pot away in its right place; [7] (and having washed and) put the (leftovers container) away in its (right) place, [and] having swept the ground, he [8] (departs ... and enters the ...).
sequences.chaya: yaḥ khalu punar teṣām āyuṣmatām paścāt pi[5]ṇḍāya ... na paryāptam bhavati *avaskārabhājanāt gr̥hītvā [6] ... . pratiśrāmayati *paridhāvanīyaghaṭam yathābhūmyām pra[7]tiśrāmayati ... yathābhūmyām pratiśrāmayitvā (BHS) pradeśam saṃmārjitvā (BHS) ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 5
sequences.translation: … together with … [If] he were to see [that the pots were empty], he would attend [to them]. The one who [9] … The one who … one … a second [10] … would spend [all night] with talk on the Dharma.
sequences.chaya: [8] ... kim saha ḏema (G) a dharmiṇi pakṣea (G) paśrujea (G) yaḥ khalu [9] ... yad re (G) bhavati ekam aṇ . (G) . ha (G) . . . śa (G) sa (G) bhavati dvitīyam [10] ... dharminyā kathayā atināmayanti ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 6
sequences.translation: [10] Then, when it was evening (time) the Blessed One, [11] (having arisen from meditation), approached (the Grove of the Gos̱iga Śāla-tree Forest). The grove keeper saw the Blessed One (coming) in the distance, [12] (and having seen him, said this to the Blessed One: “Ascetic, do not) enter this grove! Due to the fact that there are three clansmen living there, [13] (Aṇarudha, Ṇadia and Kibhira. You must not disturb them!)”
sequences.chaya: atha khalu bhagavān sāyāhnam [11] ... tena upasamakramīt (BHS) adr̥śāti (BHS) dāhapālaḥ bhagavantam dūre [12] ... praviśa etat dāham | tasmāt tatra trayaḥ kulaputrāḥ viharanti . [13] ... ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 7
sequences.translation: The Venerable Aṇarudha, withdrawn [and] in meditation [14] (with the divine ear which is purified and surpasses the human) heard (the grove keeper) conversing thus with (the Blessed One). Then, Venerable [15] (Aṇarudha said to the grove keeper: “Park keeper! Do not) obstruct (the Blessed One)! It is my Teacher, the Blessed One, [who] has arrived.”
sequences.chaya: aśrauṣīt āyuṣmān aniruddhaḥ rahogataḥ pratisaṃlīnaḥ [14] ... sārdham idam evaṃrūpam antarākathāsaṃlāpam | atha khalu āyu[15]ṣmān ... vāraya | śāstā me bhagavān anuprāptaḥ ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 8
sequences.translation: Then Ven[16]erable (Aṇarudha thus entered the park, and having entered) went to (Venerable) Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira, [17] (and having gone to them, and paid homage at their feet, sat to one side. Seated to one side, he said this: “Come out, Venerable sirs, come) out, Venerable sirs! My teacher, the Blessed One, has arrived.” (Having assented) ‘Yes, friend,’ [18] (Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira) stood up from their seats. Then Venerable Aṇarudha, Venerable [19] (Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira went out to meet the Blessed One, and said this: “Venerable sir, we bid the Blessed One welcome.” Venerable) Aṇarudha took the Blessed One’s bowl and outer robe, Venerable Ṇadia (prepared) a bed and seat for the Blessed One, [20] (and Venerable Kibhira set out water for washing feet).
sequences.chaya: atha khalu āyu[16]ṣmān ... āyuṣmān ca nandikaḥ āyuṣmān ca kibhiraḥ tena upasamakramīt (BHS) [17] ... abhikramatha āyuṣmantaḥ śāstā me bhagavān anuprāptaḥ | evam āyuṣmantaḥ iti [18] ... utthāya āsanāt | atha khalu āyuṣmān ca aniruddhaḥ āyuṣmān ca [19] ... aniruddhaḥ bhagavataḥ pātracīvaram pratyagrahīt āyuṣmān nandikaḥ bhagavataḥ śayanāsanam [20] ... ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 9
sequences.translation: (The Blessed One sat down) on the seat made perfectly ready and, having sat down, the Blessed One [21] (said this) to Venerable (Aṇarudha: “I hope you are all keeping well, Aṇarudha, I hope you are all comfortable, I hope you are) not going short (of alms-food).” [Aṇarudha:] “Yes, certainly, Venerable sir, we are all keeping well, yes, certainly we are all comfort[22]able, (yes, certainly we are not going short of alms-food).”
sequences.chaya: ... bhagavān prajñapte eva prajñapte eva āsane niṣadya ca bhagavān āyuṣma[21]ntam . ... na klāmatha | taddha vayam bhadanta asmākam kṣamaṇīyam (BHS) taddha yāpanīyam (BHS) ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 10
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] “(I hope you are all living in concord, with mutual appreciation).” [Aṇarudha:] “Yes, certainly, Venerable sir, we are all living in concord, with mutual appreciation. As to that, I (had this thought [23]: ‘It is for my gain, for my great gain, in that I make my abode with such companions in the holy life).’ Therefore, I maintain bodily acts of loving-kindness towards those venerable ones [24] (both openly and privately ... verbal acts of loving-kindness ... mental acts of loving-kindness ... Therefore, I had this thought): ‘Suppose I, having put aside my own intention, (should do what) these venerable ones wish [to do].’ [25] (Thus, Venerable sir, having put aside my own intention, I do what these venerable ones wish to do, as we are different in body), I think, but one in mind.”
sequences.chaya: [22] ... saṃmodayamānāḥ (BHS) | taddha vayam bhadanta samagrāḥ viharāmaḥ saṃmodayamānāḥ (BHS) | iha mama [23] ... | tasya mama eṣām āyuṣmatām maitram kāyakarma pratyupasthitam bhava[24]ti ... svayam abhiprāyam nikṣipetvā (BHS) eṣām āyuṣmatām cittava[25]śena ... ekam manye cittam ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 11
sequences.translation: Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira [26] (said this to the Blessed One: “Venerable sir, as to that, I had this thought: ‘It is for the gain, for the great gain) [that] I am able to make my abode (with such) companions in the holy life.’ I [27] (maintain bodily acts of loving-kindness towards those venerable ones, both openly and privately ... verbal acts of loving-kindness ... mental acts of loving-kindness. Therefore, I had the thought): ‘Suppose I, having put aside my own intention, should (do) what these venerable ones wish [to do].’ [28] (Thus, Venerable sir, having put aside my own intention, I do what these venerable ones wish to do, as we are different in body but one in mind).”
sequences.chaya: āyuṣmān eva nandikaḥ āyuṣmān eva kibhiraḥ [26] ... evaṃrūpaiḥ sabrahmacārikaiḥ sārdham nivāsam kalpāmi | tasya mama [27] ... nūnam aham svayam abhiprāyam nikṣipetvā (BHS) eṣām āyuṣmatām cittava[28]śena ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 12
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] “(Well done, well done! Aṇarudha monks! I hope, you Aṇarudha monks, while) you indeed are living (in concord thus), with mutual appreciation, that there is, surpassing the human state, (the attainment) of a distinct [29] (comfortable abiding.” [Aṇarudha:] “In this way, Venerable sir), while (we) are living (in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), there is, surpassing the human state, (the attainment of) a distinct [30] (comfortable abiding. Here, Venerable sir, whenever we want) we enter and abide in the first dhyāna, which is quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from sinful, unwholesome [31] (states, which is accompanied by thought and examination), with rapture and pleasure born (of seclusion). In this way, [32] (Venerable sir, while we indeed are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation, this is, surpassing the human state), the attainment of a distinct comfortable abiding.”
sequences.chaya: ... saṃmodayamāneṣu (BHS) viharatsu asti kaccid uttarim manuṣyadharmāt viśeṣasparśavihāraḥ (BHS) [29] ... saṃmodayamāneṣu (BHS) viharatsu asti uttarim manuṣyadharmāt viśeṣa[30]sparśavihāraḥ (BHS) ... viviktam eva kāmaiḥ viviktam pāpakaiḥ akuśalaiḥ [31] ... vivekajam prītisukham prathamam dhyānam upasampadya viharāmaḥ | idam [32] ... viśeṣasparśavihāraḥ (BHS) adhigataḥ ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 13
sequences.translation: [Buddha]: “Well done, well done! Aṇarudha [monks]! [33] (I hope, you Aṇarudha monks), by surmounting (that abiding), by making [that abiding] subside, surpassing the human (state), that there is (the attainment) of another [34] (distinct comfortable abiding?” [Aṇarudha:] “In this way, Venerable sir), while (we) are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation [35] (by surmounting that abiding, by making that abiding subside, there is, surpassing the human state), the attainment of (another) distinct comfortable abiding. Here, Venerable sir, whenever we [36] (want, by the subsiding of thought and examination, on account of internal placidity, and) the unification (of mind, we enter and abide) in the second dhyāna, which is without thought, without examination [and] has rapture and pleasure that is concentrated. [37] (In this way, Venerable sir), while (we) are living (in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), by surmounting that abiding, [38] (by making that abiding subside, this is, surpassing the human state), the attainment of (another distinct comfortable abiding).”
sequences.chaya: sādhu sādhu aniruddhāḥ | [33] ... samatikramāya pratipraśrabdhyai asti anyaḥ uttarim ma[34]nuṣyadharmāt | ... samagreṣu evam saṃmodayamāneṣu (BHS) viharatsu asya [35] ... viśeṣasparśavihāraḥ (BHS) adhigataḥ | iha vayam bhadanta yā[36]vat ... ekotibhāvam avitarkam avicāram samāhitam prītisukham dvitīyam dhyā[37]nam ... saṃmodayamāneṣu viharatsu asya vihārasya samatikramāya [38] ... adhigataḥ ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 14
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] “Well done! Aṇarudha [monks]! I hope, [you] Aṇarudha [monks], while you (are living) in concord thus, with mutual appreciation, [39] (by surmounting that abiding, by making that abiding subside, that there is), surpassing the human state, (the attainment) of another distinct comfortable abiding?” [Aṇarudha:] [40] (In this way, Venerable sir, while we indeed are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), by surmounting that abiding, by making [that abiding] subside, [41] (there is, surpassing the human state, the attainment of another distinct comfortable abiding. Here), Venerable sir, whenever (we) want, with the fading away as well of rapture, (we abide) having equanimity, [42] (being mindful and clearly comprehending, we experience happiness with the body. Such that the noble ones) declare: 'He has equanimity, is mindful, is one who abides happily.' (We) enter and (abide) in the third dhyāna. [43] (In this way, Venerable sir, while we indeed are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), by surmounting that abiding, by making [that abiding] subside, this is, surpassing the [44] (human state, the attainment of) another (distinct comfortable abiding)."
sequences.chaya: sādhu aniruddhāḥ | kaccid aniruddhāḥ yuṣmāsu eva samagreṣu evam saṃmo[39]dayamāneṣu ... anyaḥ uttarim manuṣyadharmāt viśeṣasparśavihāraḥ (BHS) [40] ... viharatsu asya vihārasya samatikramāya pratipraśrabdhyai [41] ... vayam bhadanta yāvat eva ākāṅkṣāmaḥ prītyāḥ ca virāgāt upe[42]kṣakāḥ ... ācakṣante upekṣakaḥ smr̥timān sukhavihārī [iti] | tr̥tīyam dhyānam upasampadya [43] ... asya vihārasya samatikramāya pratipraśrabdhyai ayam anyaḥ uttarim [44] ... ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 15
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] … [Aṇarudha:] "… (as far as) the fourth dhyāna [and] thus, as far as …"
sequences.chaya: ... caturtham dhyānam evam yāvat evam ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 16
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] “Well done! Aṇarudha [monks]! I hope, [you] Aṇarudha [monks], while you are [45] (living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), by surmounting (that) abiding, by making [that abiding] subside, surpassing (the human state), that there is another [46] (distinct comfortable abiding, which has been attained?” [Aṇarudha:] “In this way, Venerable sir, while we) are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation, there is, [47] (surpassing the human state, the attainment of) another (distinct comfortable abiding. Here, Venerable sir), whenever we want, with the complete surmounting of perceptions of forms, [48] (with the passing away of perceptions of sensory impingement, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity), perceiving ‘space is infinite,’ we enter and abide in the base of the end of space. [49] (In this way, Venerable sir, while we indeed are living in concord thus, with mutual appreciation), by surmounting that abiding, by making [that abiding] subside, this is, [50] (surpassing the human state, the attainment of) another (distinct comfortable abiding).”
sequences.chaya: sādhu aniruddhāḥ | kaccid punar yuṣmāsu eva [45] ... asya vihārasya samatikramāya ratipraśrabdhyai asti anyaḥ utta[46]rim ... asmāsu eva samagreṣu evam saṃmodayamāneṣu viharatsu asti anyaḥ [47] ... yāvat eva ākāṅkṣāmaḥ sarvaśaḥ rūpasaṃjñānām samatikramāt [48] ... anantaḥ ākāśaḥ iti ākāśānantyāyatanam upasampadya viharā[49]maḥ | ... asya vihārasya samatikramāya pratipraśrabdhyai ayam anyaḥ [50] ... ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 17
sequences.translation: [Buddha:] ... [Aṇarudha:] (... as far as) “By completely surmounting the base of neither-perception-nor-non-perception, (we enter upon and abide) in the cessation of perception and feeling. [51] (By seeing with wisdom, we are ones whose taints are destroyed.” … as far as [Buddha:] “Well done, well done, Aṇarudha! There is not) beyond that a higher comfortable abiding.”
sequences.chaya: ... sarvaśaḥ naivasaṃjñāsaṃjñāyatanam samatikramya saṃjñāvedayitani[51]rodham ... tataḥ uttaritaraḥ sparśavihāraḥ (BHS) ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 18
sequences.translation: Then the Blessed One instructed, urged, roused [and] (gladdened) Venerable Aṇarudha, [52] (Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira) with a talk on the Dharma. [53] (Then, the Blessed One, having instructed, urged,) roused [and] gladdened (those venerable ones), [and] having arisen from his seat, departed.
sequences.chaya: atha khalu bhagavān āyuṣmantam ca aniruddham [52] ... dharmyā kathayā saṃdarśayati (BHS) samādāpayati (BHS) samuttejayati (BHS) | [53] ... samuttejayitvā (BHS) saṃpraharṣayitvā (BHS) utthāya āsanāt prākramīt ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 19
sequences.translation: [54] (Then Venerable Aṇarudha, Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira), having accompanied the Blessed One for a short while, and so returning from there [55] (Venerable Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira approached Venerable Aṇarudha, and having approached him paid homage to his feet), sat down (to one side). Seated to one side, Venerable [56] (Ṇadia and Venerable Kibhira said this to Venerable Aṇarudha): “Have we ever, having approached you, (reported) this (matter) to the Venerable Aṇarudha [57]: ‘(We, dwelling with these various attainments), have our taints destroyed by our seeing with wisdom,’ such that, [58] the Venerable (Aṇarudha in the Blessed One’s presence revealed about us, as far as the destruction of the taints)?”
sequences.chaya: [54] ... bhagavantam muhūrtam anubadhya tataḥ eva pratinivr̥tya [55] ... niṣīdensuḥ (BHS) ekam antam nisinnāḥ | āyuṣmān [56] ... nu vayam āyuṣmataḥ aniruddhasya upasaṃkramya etam [57] ... viharantaḥ prājñayā dr̥ṣṭvā āsravāḥ ca parikṣīṇāḥ tad idaṃ (BHS) āyu[58]ṣmān ... ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 20
sequences.translation: [Aṇarudha:] “[You] venerable ones, having (never) approached me and reported this matter: [59] ‘(We, dwelling with these various attainments, have our taints) destroyed (by our seeing with wisdom).’ For a long time the venerable ones’ [60] (thoughts were known) by me using [my very own] mind: ‘(You venerable ones, dwelling with these various attainments), have your taints destroyed by your seeing with wisdom.’ The deities, [61] (having approached me, reported this matter) to me: ‘(Those venerable ones, dwelling with these various) attainments, have [their] taints destroyed by seeing with wisdom.’ [62] (Such that, I in the Blessed One’s presence revealed about you, as far as the destruction of the taints).”
sequences.chaya: ... āyuṣmantaḥ vo (G) traya (G) upasaṃkramya etam artham ārocesuṃ (BHS) | [59] ... parikṣīṇāḥ | dīrgharātram mayā āyuṣmataḥ cetasā cetasā [60] ... viharantaḥ prājñayā dr̥ṣṭvā āsravāh parikṣīṇāḥ | devatāḥ me u[61]pasaṃkramya ... samāpattibhiḥ viharantaḥ prājñayā dr̥ṣṭvā āsravāh parikṣīṇāḥ [62] ... prakāśayī (BHS) iti ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 21
sequences.translation: Then the Blessed One, having gone by the road for a short while, (and having left) the road, [63] sat down (near the base of a certain tree) on the very seat prepared [for him]. Then the spirit Driga [64] (approached the Blessed One. Having approached) [him], having paid homage (to the feet of the Blessed One), stood to one side. Standing to one side, (the deity) Driga [65] (said this to the Blessed One: “It is for the gain of the Vṛji people, for the great gain of the Vṛji people), in that these three clansmen, Aṇarudha, Ṇadia and Kibhira, live there.” [Buddha:] [66] “(It is so, Driga, it is so! It is for the gain of the Vṛji people, for the great gain of the Vṛji people) in that these three clansmen, Aṇarudha, Ṇadia and Kibhira, live there.
sequences.chaya: atha khalu bhagavān muhūrtam mārgeṇa gatvā mārgāt [63] ... prajñapte eva āsane nyasīdat | atha khalu dīrghā de[64]vatā ... vanditvā ekam antam asthāsi (BHS) | ekam antam sthitā ca dīrghā [65] ... trayaḥ kulaputrāḥ viharanti aniruddhaḥ nandikaḥ kibhiraḥ | [66] ... yatra ete trayaḥ kulaputrāḥ viharanti aniruddhaḥ nandikaḥ kibhiraḥ ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 22
sequences.translation: [67] (If the clan from which these three clansmen went forth from the home life into homelessness) remember these three clansmen with a confident heart, [then] for this clan [68] (... for a long time that would be for their welfare) and happiness. [If] the [extended] family circle from which … [If] the village from which … [69] (If the town from which … If the country from which these three clansmen went forth from the home life into homelessness remember these three clansmen with a confident heart, then for that country that would lead to its welfare and) happiness (for a long time).
sequences.chaya: [67] ... prasannacittam ete trīn kulaputrān anusmareyuḥ asmai ku[68]lāya ... sukhāya | yasmāt kulaparivartāt yasmāt grāmāt yasmāt ni[69]gamāt ... sukhāya ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 23
sequences.translation: In which case, if the world as well, with its gods, its Māras, and its Brāhmas, [70] (and this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its devas and its humans) should remember (these three clansmen) with a confident heart, [then] for the world with its gods, its Māras, [71] (and its Brāhmas, and for this population with its ascetics and brahmins), its gods and its humans, that would lead to their welfare and happiness for a long time.”
sequences.chaya: yatra sadevakaḥ eva lokaḥ samārakaḥ sabrahma[70]kaḥ ... prasannacittam anusmareyuḥ sadevakasya lokasya samāra[71]kasya ... sadevamānuṣā syāt dīrgharātram hitāya sukhāya ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 24
sequences.translation: Then [72] (having heard the utterance of the spirit Driga, the earth gods raised their voices and proclaimed the utterance): “It is for the gain of the Vṛji people, for the great gain of the Vṛji people in that these three clansmen [73] (Aṇarudha, Ṇadia and Kibhira live here.” … the gods of the Four Great Kings … gods of the heaven of the Thirty)-three … the Yāma gods … the gods of the Tuṣita [heaven] … the gods who delight in creating [74] … (the gods who delight in others’ creations … The gods of Brahmā’s retinue) heard the utterance (of the gods who delight in others’ creations) [and] raised their voices [and] proclaimed the utterance: “…” Thus, those [75] (venerable ones at that moment, in a second, in an instant) were announced (as far as the Brahmā) world.”
sequences.chaya: atha [72] ... lābhaḥ vr̥jīnām sulabdhalābhaḥ vr̥jīnām yatra ete trayaḥ kulapu[73]trāḥ ... trayatriṃśantaḥ yāmāḥ devāḥ tuṣitāh devāḥ nirmāṇaratayaḥ devāḥ [74] ... ghoṣam śrutvā śabdam akarensuḥ (BHS) ghoṣam anuśrāvayensuḥ (BHS) iti te [75] ... brahmalokāt pratisaṃviditāḥ abhūvan ||

sequences.label: Paragraph 25
sequences.translation: That is what the Blessed One said. Pleased, [76] (the spirit Driga rejoiced at the words of the Blessed One).
sequences.chaya: idam avacat bhagavān āttamanāḥ ||
